name: La Cervatina – circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)
name_original: La Cervatina – circular desde Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)
region_intro: Hiking trails around Puebla De Lillo are situated within the Regional Park of Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, offering diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region features mountainous terrain with peaks like Peña Susarón and Pico Yordas, alongside verdant valleys. Ancient forests such as the Pinar de Lillo, home to Scots pine, beech, and oak, characterize much of the area. The Porma River and glacial lakes like Lago Isoba and Lago Ausente further define the natural environment. Best hiking trails around Puebla De Lillo The most popular hiking route is Pico Susarón from Puebla de Lillo – Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route ascends to Peña Susarón, offering views within the regional park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Entrevalles and the Pinzón Valley from Isoba - circular through the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park (PRC-LE 27), a moderate 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This circular route explores the Pinzón Valley and areas near Lago Isoba within the regional park. Local hikers also love the La Cervatina – circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28), a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Puebla De Lillo is defined by mountainous terrain, ancient pine and beech forests, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to longer circular paths. The routes in Puebla De Lillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De Lillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Puebla de Lillo offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various difficulty levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents, all set within the stunning landscapes of the Regional Park of Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Yes, Puebla de Lillo has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050275436/mirador-de-la-pena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peña Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, offering scenic views without significant elevation gain. There are 4 easy routes in total.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Puebla de Lillo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Notable circular routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050675260/entrevalles-and-the-pinzon-valley-from-isoba-circular-through-the-riano-and-mampodre-mountain-regional-park-prc-le-27" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrevalles and the Pinzón Valley from Isoba&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047618329/la-cervatina-circular-from-puebla-de-lillo-pr-le-28" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cervatina – circular from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, which explore varied landscapes within the regional park.

Q: What kind of natural attractions, like lakes or waterfalls, can I see while hiking in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore glacial lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Isoba&lt;/a&gt; and Lago Ausente, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porma Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features natural waterfalls like Cascada de los Forfogones and the route to Cascada del Tabayon del Mongayu, adding to the scenic appeal of the trails.

Q: Are there any trails in Puebla de Lillo that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, many trails in Puebla de Lillo lead to stunning viewpoints. Hikes to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Yordas (1,967 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Peak (1,878 m)&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, including glimpses of the Picos de Europa and the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Is Puebla de Lillo a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Puebla de Lillo is well-suited for families, offering trails of varying lengths and difficulties. Many routes are accessible and lead to natural wonders like Lago Isoba, providing engaging experiences for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050275436/mirador-de-la-pena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peña Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of an easy, family-friendly option.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Puebla de Lillo area, especially within the Regional Park of Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access to protected natural spaces.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The best time for hiking in Puebla de Lillo is typically from spring to autumn. Spring brings vibrant wildflowers, while autumn offers spectacular golden and red foliage. Summer provides pleasant temperatures for higher altitude trails. Winter transforms the landscape into a snowy wonderland, suitable for snowshoeing or skiing near Puerto de San Isidro, but requires different gear and experience.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting hiking trails in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Puebla de Lillo, as well as at the starting points of many popular trails. Specific parking areas are often designated near trailheads, particularly for routes leading to attractions like Lago Isoba or the Pinar de Lillo. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The mountainous environment of Puebla de Lillo supports diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot deer, wild boars, foxes, badgers, and chamois. The region is also home to rare birds like the capercaillie. While shy, brown bears and wolves occasionally inhabit the more remote areas, making wildlife spotting a unique experience.

Q: Are there any unique forests to explore in the Puebla de Lillo area?
A: Yes, Puebla de Lillo is home to the Pinar de Lillo, a rare indigenous Scots pine forest on the Iberian Peninsula. This ancient forest features trees over 150 years old, alongside beech, oak, rowan, and birch, offering a unique and serene hiking experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The hiking routes in Puebla de Lillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountainous terrain, the beauty of the ancient pine and beech forests, and the tranquility of the glacial lakes, highlighting the diverse and rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Puebla de Lillo offers cultural points of interest. You can visit the medieval Torreón de Puebla de Lillo, a 14th-century watchtower that now serves as a Cultural and Interpretation Center. Other historical sites include the 18th-century Ermita de la Virgen de las Nieves and the 16th-century San Vicente parish church, often accessible as detours from hiking routes.
